reasoning: Emotional (60%): Strong Zelda outdoor ruins feel — warm golden-tan stone patches on vivid organic green reads immediately as overgrown ruins. Palette is bright, warm, and Hyrule-appropriate; no muddy or realistic-photo tones. Organic shape variation in the patches is hand-pixeled and toy-diorama in spirit. Score ~8/10 emotional. Technical (40%): Pixel placement is deliberate, no gradient bleed, no AA, hard edges throughout. Seamless edge continuity is clean. However, the 2x2 tiled view reveals noticeable repetition — the golden-tan patches cluster in recognizable positions across tile repeats, breaking the illusion of organic variation. Additionally, the stone/ruin patches read as a single flat tone with no 2-tone hard shading to give macro-feature depth. Score ~6/10 technical. Weighted: 0.6*8 + 0.4*6 = 7.2 → 7. Marginal: passes emotional bar but tiling repetition and flat stone shading need revision before production use.
